constraints: Vitest dependencies are declared but not installed in the release environment

observed.statement: provider.cancel executes before local cancelled state is saved

inferred.statement: a timeout after remote completion permits duplicate remote cancellation on retry

assumed.statement: provider timeout may occur after remote commit
assumed.consequence: requires an idempotency mechanism or reconciliation

impact_map: API caller -> authorization -> cancellation service -> billing provider -> repository -> event publisher

invariants.statement: one logical cancellation causes at most one remote cancellation and one event
a cross-tenant request changes no state and performs no downstream call

risks.statement: ambiguous provider timeout -> retry repeats remote cancellation -> duplicate billing-side effect
cross-tenant identifier -> unauthorized cancellation -> tenant boundary violation

scenarios.title: ambiguous provider timeout and retry
preconditions: first provider call commits remotely then times out

findings.statement: remote effect precedes durable idempotency state, leaving an ambiguous-timeout duplicate window
findings.next_action: introduce provider idempotency/reconciliation and execute the repeated-delivery test

residual_risks.statement: provider sandbox idempotency semantics are not supplied
residual_risks.revisit_condition: provider contract or sandbox evidence available

findings: NOT_READY is supported by the critical ordering defect; execution remains blocked
